meaning: n.反向气流,强硬的抗议,猛烈的反应
examples: Other experts delivered a strong counterblast to the professor's claims.
The sparseness of the Nano is in some ways a welcome counterblast to costly add-ons found in the west, where cars are becoming almost as intelligent as their drivers.
But it is more than that: it is also a counterblast.
This counterblast against consultants is largely to the good.
Although he does not mention religion, Mr Stannard's tome is carefully timed as a counterblast to the book by Stephen Hawking and Leonard Mlodinow that rejects the need for the existence of God.
